label: PASS
rationale: Range1 had read/write locking enabled, ReadLocked and WriteLocked false, and LockOnReset {0,1}. Opal defines Hardware as reset type 1, and LockOnReset values that match the reset set ReadLocked and WriteLocked to True; the returned true/true state is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range1 had LockOnReset {0,1}; Hardware is reset type 1, so the reset matched and should have set ReadLocked and WriteLocked to True. Returning false/false is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Range1 had LockOnReset {0,3} and Hardware reset is type 1, so the reset did not match. The state machine preserves the prior false ReadLocked and WriteLocked values on a nonmatching reset; the false/false result is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range1 had LockOnReset {0,3}, while Hardware reset is type 1. Because the reset did not match, ReadLocked and WriteLocked should have remained false, not changed to true/true.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was set true, enabling TPER_RESET. Opal says an enabled TPER_RESET sets ReadLocked and WriteLocked true for Locking objects whose LockOnReset contains Programmatic, and {0,3} contains reset type 3; the true/true result is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable enabled TPER_RESET, and Range1 LockOnReset {0,3} contains Programmatic reset type 3. TPER_RESET therefore should set ReadLocked and WriteLocked true, so returning false/false is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Range1 was already read/write locked and LockOnReset was the empty set. The documents say an empty set means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset, so true/true after the power cycle is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range1 was already read/write locked and LockOnReset was empty. An empty LockOnReset preserves the current lock state through resets, so changing the returned lock bits to false/false is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Range1 had ReadLocked and WriteLocked false with an empty LockOnReset. Empty LockOnReset means the lock bits do not change on any reset, so false/false after HardwareReset is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range1 had ReadLocked and WriteLocked false and an empty LockOnReset, which preserves the current lock state on reset. Returning true/true after HardwareReset is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Although PowerCycle with LockOnReset {0} leaves the WriteLocked cell true, WriteLockEnabled was false. The spec says WriteLocked is ignored when WriteLockEnabled is false, so a write to the range may succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: WriteLockEnabled was false, so the WriteLocked value is ignored and the write-locking feature is disabled for the range. Rejecting the write as INVALID_PARAMETER is not supported by the locking-state rules.

label: FAIL
rationale: Power Cycle is reset type 0 and LockOnReset {0} would set WriteLocked true, but the power cycle occurred while the Locking SP session was still open and no new StartSession appears before the target Get. Core says power cycles abort all open sessions, so a successful Get in that aborted session is not protocol-compliant.
